environment.note: Services are not running in this environment; reporting based on code analysis and configuration.

database.flyway_status: V0-V62 active (63 total). Next migration = V63
database.details: Flyway migrations are configured and locked in. Neon PostgreSQL used for development instead of local docker postgres service.

roles: Permission cache (loaded via SecurityService.getCachedPermissions() in JwtAuthenticationFilter)
redis.details: Critical component for RBAC and rate limiting. Not verifiable without Docker.

topics.name: nu-aura.fluence-content
topics.description: NU-Fluence content indexing for Elasticsearch search

kafka.details: Mission-critical for async workflows (approval events, notifications, audit). 5 topics + 5 DLT topics. Manual commits configured for exactly-once semantics.

elasticsearch.expected_check: GET /actuator/health -> elasticsearch component OR GET http://localhost:9200/_cluster/health
roles: Full-text search for NU-Fluence (knowledge base, wiki, blogs)
Content indexing via Kafka consumer (FluenceSearchConsumer listening to nu-aura.fluence-content)
elasticsearch.details: Optional for core HRMS but required for NU-Fluence Phase 2. Index health critical for knowledge base search.

minio.details: Required for document management. Stores employee files, contract PDFs, attachments.

websocket.protocol: STOMP over WebSocket with SockJS fallback
websocket.security_note: Never uses wildcard '*' in production (CSWSH risk)
roles: Live notification delivery to connected clients
websocket.details: Critical for real-time user notifications. SockJS provides fallback for environments without WebSocket support.

actuator.expected_response: JSON with status + component health (db, redis, kafka, elasticsearch, diskSpace, ping)
actuator.details: Health endpoint aggregates all component statuses. Flyway endpoint shows migration history.

scheduled_jobs.tenant_isolation: Per-tenant isolation. Iterates all ACTIVE tenants, wraps each in try-catch, clears TenantContext in finally
scheduled_jobs.error_handling: EXCELLENT - Tenant isolation prevents one tenant error from blocking others
scheduled_jobs.lazy_initialization_risk: NONE - Uses repository queries, no lazy-loaded associations
scheduled_jobs.details: Escalates stale approval requests. Checks escalation config, resolves target approver, creates new escalated step, sends notifications.

responsibilities: Escalates overdue approval steps to configured targets
Auto-approves or auto-rejects based on timeout config
Sends reminder notifications for nearing-deadline approvals
scheduled_jobs.details: Ensures approval workflows don't get stuck indefinitely. P0 stability concern.

methods.name: sendBirthdayEmails
methods.fix_applied: R2-003 FIX: Now iterates all ACTIVE tenants with TenantContext

methods.name: sendAnniversaryEmails
methods.fix_applied: R2-017 FIX: Uses ChronoUnit.YEARS.between() instead of year subtraction for leap year correctness

methods.name: retryFailedEmails
methods.fix_applied: SEC-FIX: Now iterates tenants with TenantContext (was cross-tenant query without tenant_id filter)

methods.name: sendScheduledEmails
methods.fix_applied: SEC-FIX: Now iterates tenants with TenantContext

scheduled_jobs.tenant_isolation: ALL methods now properly iterate tenants and set TenantContext
scheduled_jobs.details: Birthday/anniversary/scheduled emails with proper multi-tenant isolation.

scheduled_jobs.tenant_isolation: Per-tenant with try-catch isolation and TenantContext clearing in finally block
scheduled_jobs.error_handling: Excellent - one tenant error doesn't block others
responsibilities: Auto-Expire: Marks active contracts past end date as EXPIRED
Auto-Renew: Renews auto-renewable contracts past end date
Reminder Creation: Creates idempotent reminders (30, 15, 7 days before expiry)
Notification Dispatch: Sends notifications for due reminders
scheduled_jobs.idempotency: Unique partial index on (contract_id, reminder_type, reminder_date) WHERE is_completed=false prevents duplicates
scheduled_jobs.details: Comprehensive contract lifecycle automation with multi-tenant isolation and idempotency protection.
Attendance automation: regularizes attendance and auto-approves compensatory off.
Scheduled notifications for digests, daily summaries, and reminders.
Job board sync for NU-Hire recruitment module.
Retries failed webhook deliveries. High-frequency job.
Health check for Redis (rate limiting backend). Frequent monitoring.
Cleanup of expired rate-limit buckets in Redis.
Executes scheduled reports and emails them to recipients. Runs every minute (high frequency).

findings.description: Docker unavailable in current environment. Unable to verify running state of infrastructure services (Redis, Kafka, Elasticsearch, MinIO).
findings.impact: Health checks cannot be executed; all service status marked as UNABLE_TO_VERIFY
findings.recommendation: Run health checks in an environment with Docker and running backend service: 'curl -s http://localhost:8080/actuator/health | jq .'

findings.description: ScheduledReportExecutionJob runs every minute (cron='0 * * * * *'). This is very high frequency and could become a bottleneck.
findings.recommendation: Consider increasing interval to 5 or 15 minutes unless sub-minute execution is critical. Monitor query performance and database load.

findings.description: WebhookDeliveryService runs every 60 seconds (fixedRate=60000). High frequency job.
findings.impact: May cause database contention if many webhooks are pending
findings.recommendation: Monitor webhook queue depth and adjust interval if needed

findings.description: RateLimitingFilter.checkRedisHealth() runs every 30 seconds. Frequent health checks are good for early detection but add overhead.
findings.details: This is by design to detect Redis failures quickly

findings.description: Tenant isolation excellent across all scheduled jobs. All jobs that iterate tenants now use TenantContext properly.
findings.impact: Data isolation is strong. No cross-tenant data leaks detected in scheduled job code.
fixes_applied: R2-003 FIX: EmailSchedulerService birthday/anniversary now iterate tenants
R2-017 FIX: EmailSchedulerService anniversary uses ChronoUnit.YEARS for leap year correctness
SEC-FIX: EmailSchedulerService retry/scheduled now iterate tenants with TenantContext

findings.description: Kafka configured with manual commit mode (listener.ack-mode=manual) and idempotent producers. Excellent for exactly-once semantics.
findings.details: Consumer enable_auto_commit=false ensures manual control. Producer idempotence prevents duplicate messages.

findings.description: WebSocket configuration does NOT use wildcard origins in allowed list. CSWSH protection in place.
Flyway migrations V0-V62 active (63 total). Next migration = V63. Legacy Liquibase in db/changelog/ is present but NOT used.
findings.warning: Do not use legacy Liquibase directory. All migrations must go through Flyway.

findings.description: ApprovalEscalationJob and ContractLifecycleScheduler use @Transactional on private helper methods. This is supported by Spring but unusual.
findings.impact: Works correctly but less transparent than marking public methods @Transactional
findings.recommendation: Consider moving @Transactional to public entry point (processEscalations/processContractLifecycle) instead of private methods

findings.description: Elasticsearch is optional for core HRMS but required for NU-Fluence Phase 2 (knowledge base search).
findings.phase: Phase 2: Backend built, frontend routes defined, UI not started
findings.details: FluenceSearchConsumer listens to nu-aura.fluence-content topic and indexes to Elasticsearch

summary.reason: Backend service not running; Docker unavailable for container health checks

recommendations.wait_for: All containers to reach healthy state (watch 'docker-compose ps')

recommendations.expected: All components (db, redis, kafka, elasticsearch, diskSpace, ping) should show UP
All 10 topics (5 + 5 DLT) should be listed

recommendations.details: Verify all 18 scheduled job instances are registered
ScheduledReportExecutionJob running every minute is high frequency. Consider if this is necessary or if 5-15 min interval is acceptable.

next_steps: Start Docker and verify all containers reach healthy state
Run health check against actuator endpoint to verify all components are UP
Review ScheduledReportExecutionJob frequency (every minute is high)
Monitor Kafka consumer lag to ensure no backlog buildup
Verify WebSocket real-time notifications are flowing correctly
Check Redis memory usage for permission cache and rate limiting
Monitor scheduled job execution times to detect performance regressions
Validate tenant isolation in all multi-tenant scheduled jobs (currently OK based on code review)
